_comment: Murata GRM/GCM/GJM-series part-number grammar. Variable length 13-16 chars depending on trailing revision/packaging suffix. Source: Murata FAQ + Mouser global part numbering doc + worked example GRM21BR71H474KA88L.
_tcc_note: Murata uses 2-char TCC codes. Verified against real parts: 5C=C0G (GRM2165C2A103J), R6=X5R (GRM21BR61E106K), R7=X7R (GRM21BR71H475K), C7=X7S (GCM21BC71E106KE36L), R9=X8R (GCM21BR91H104KA37L), L8=X8L. Common pitfalls: C7 is NOT C0G (that's 5C only); X8R is R9 not R8 in modern Murata coding; R5 is not used (X5R is R6, not R5). JIS-style codes (B1/B3/F1/R1/R3) appear in older / Japan-market parts.
_jis_only_codes: B1/B3=JIS B (±10/-30%, -25..85C), F1=JIS F (Z5U-like), R1/R3=JIS R. Decode but don't map to EIA class — these appear only in Japan-market legacy parts.
